items.title: Anker 10-port flat plug power strip now down at $20 via Amazon
items.summary:
Anker Flat Plug Power Strip Surge Protector

Buy for $20 (Reg. $26)

What we love

If you need to extend your power options, these simple and yet effective Anker models are a solid choice with a slim-line body and an ultra-flat plug you can easily sneak behind furniture. This one has 6 AC outlets, a pair of USB-C jacks, and I like having a pair of legacy USB-A options handy like this for folks with older devices, and things that still make use of the older standard. The model with the 5-foot lead is $20 and the 20-foot option is down at $27 right now.

items.title: Apple’s failed self-driving car program left a legacy of powerful AI chips

items.title: 9to5Toys News Weekly recap – new LEGO Pokémon, Donkey Kong leak, 96mm Tetraprism iPhone Lens, more
items.summary: Welcome to 9to5Toys News Weekly – your go-to spot to catch up on the latest new product releases, features, and hands-on reviews. This weekly roundup collects all of the coolest new gadgets, the most interesting updates in the world of tech, and more from the past week – Nintendo x LEGO Donkey Kong Arcade set has leaked, LEGO has officially revealed the first Pokémon sets with minifigs (pre-orders now live), SANDMARC debuts new 96mm Tetraprism iPhone Lens, you can now score some FREE credit ahead of the Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Galaxy Watch 9 launch, and more.

The deals start from $599 on the base Pixel 10 and you will find the Pixel 10 Pro at a solid $300 off the list price in brand new, unlocked condition. There are price drops, however, now live on all models in the lineup: Pixel 10, Pixel 10 Pro, 10 Pro XL, and Pixel 10 Pro Fold. Check it out in detail below.

  • Amazon also has 42mm and 46mm Apple Watch Series 11 models down at $299 and $329, respectively, as of this morning.

items.title: Reed Jobs would rather talk about curing cancer than his last name
items.summary: When we last sat down with Jobs at TechCrunch Disrupt nearly three years ago, his firm Yosemite was brand new and biotech was still reeling from its post-pandemic crash. Now, the venture outfit has a team of 17; a cluster of blockbuster drugs are all losing patent protection in roughly the same window, creating all kinds of new opportunities; and AI has gone from a curiosity to, in Jobs's words, a huge part of what Yosemite does. "I didn't expect Yosemite to be moving this fast," he said.
